common.mergeMode.videoJoin.timelineHintT: Video join mode is active. Only video clips can be added to the video track.
common.mergeMode.videoJoin.videoTrackEmp: Click a video file in the media list to add it to the video track.
common.mergeMode.videoJoin.audioTrackEmp: This mode focuses on video joining, so the audio track is not part of the arrangement.
common.mergeMode.videoJoin.rejectAudioIn: Video join mode is active. Choose a video clip for the video track.
common.mergeMode.audioJoin.timelineHintT: Audio join mode is active. Only audio clips can be added to the audio track.
common.mergeMode.audioJoin.videoTrackEmp: This mode focuses on audio joining, so the video track is not part of the arrangement.
common.mergeMode.audioJoin.audioTrackEmp: Click an audio file in the media list to add it to the audio track.
common.mergeMode.audioJoin.rejectVideoIn: Audio join mode is active. Choose an audio clip for the audio track.
common.mergeMode.audioVideoCompose.timel: Audio-video compose mode is active. Add one video and one audio file separately.
common.mergeMode.audioVideoCompose.video: Click a video file in the media list to place it on the video track. Adding another one replaces the current video automatically.
common.mergeMode.audioVideoCompose.audio: Click an audio file in the media list to place it on the audio track. Adding another one replaces the current audio automatically.
common.processingMode.videoConvert.descr: Keeps the original video and audio streams whenever possible and transcodes automatically only when the target container needs compatible codecs.
Exports only the video track without audio and converts to a compatible video codec automatically for specific containers.
Extracts the first audio track by default and writes it as a standalone file in the selected output format.
common.processingMode.subtitleTrackExtra: Extracts the first subtitle track by default. Text subtitles are exported in the target format, while image subtitles are best exported as MKS to preserve the original encoding.
common.splitAudio.acceleration.cpu.descr: Always uses the bundled CPU runtime for separation for maximum compatibility on any machine.
common.splitAudio.acceleration.gpuPrefer: Tries a discrete GPU first. NVIDIA GPUs prefer CUDA, other GPUs use DirectML, and the workflow falls back to the CPU automatically if no GPU is available.
common.outputFormat.video.mp4.descriptio: Best compatibility for common players and mobile devices.
common.outputFormat.video.mkv.descriptio: A more flexible container that is better for preserving original codecs and long-form video assets.
common.outputFormat.video.mov.descriptio: Works well with some editing tools and Apple workflows.
common.outputFormat.video.avi.descriptio: A traditional video container suited for older software or basic compatibility scenarios.
common.outputFormat.video.wmv.descriptio: Fits Windows environments and some legacy playback scenarios.
common.outputFormat.video.m4v.descriptio: Keeps MP4-style workflows and fits some mobile devices and Apple ecosystems.
common.outputFormat.video.flv.descriptio: Useful for older streaming stacks or platforms that still expect FLV.
common.outputFormat.video.webm.descripti: Designed for web and browser scenarios where open media formats matter more.
common.outputFormat.video.ts.description: Well suited for streaming containers or broadcast-oriented assets.
common.outputFormat.video.m2ts.descripti: Works well for Blu-ray or high-bitrate transport scenarios.
common.outputFormat.video.mpeg.descripti: Fits traditional video workflows and some broadcast reference scenarios.
common.outputFormat.video.mpg.descriptio: A common MPEG container extension for workflows that specifically need the .mpg suffix.
common.outputFormat.trim.mp4.description: Best compatibility for common players and mobile devices.
common.outputFormat.trim.mkv.description: A more flexible container that is better for preserving high-quality trimmed clips.
common.outputFormat.trim.mov.description: Works well with some editing tools and Apple workflows.
common.outputFormat.trim.avi.description: A traditional video container suited for older software or basic compatibility scenarios.
common.outputFormat.trim.wmv.description: Fits Windows environments and some legacy playback scenarios.
common.outputFormat.trim.m4v.description: Keeps MP4-style workflows and fits some mobile devices and Apple ecosystems.
common.outputFormat.trim.flv.description: Useful for older streaming stacks or platforms that still expect FLV.
common.outputFormat.trim.webm.descriptio: Designed for web and browser scenarios where open media distribution matters.
common.outputFormat.trim.ts.description: Well suited for streaming containers or broadcast-side clip output.
common.outputFormat.trim.m2ts.descriptio: Works well for Blu-ray or high-bitrate transport clips.
common.outputFormat.trim.mpeg.descriptio: Fits traditional video workflows and some broadcast reference scenarios.
common.outputFormat.trim.mpg.description: A common MPEG container extension for workflows that specifically need the .mpg suffix.
common.outputFormat.audio.mp3.descriptio: A general-purpose audio format with broad compatibility.
common.outputFormat.audio.m4a.descriptio: An AAC-based container with a good balance of quality and size.
common.outputFormat.audio.wav.descriptio: Uncompressed audio that is convenient for further editing.
common.outputFormat.audio.flac.descripti: Lossless compressed audio that works well for archiving.
common.outputFormat.audio.wma.descriptio: Fits some Windows playback environments and legacy workflows.
common.outputFormat.audio.ogg.descriptio: An open audio container that works well for Vorbis distribution.
common.outputFormat.audio.opus.descripti: A modern format with high quality at low bitrates, especially for voice and streaming.
common.outputFormat.audio.aiff.descripti: Uncompressed audio commonly seen in some professional audio and Apple workflows.
common.outputFormat.audio.aif.descriptio: A common AIFF extension for workflows that specifically need the .aif suffix.
common.outputFormat.audio.mka.descriptio: A Matroska Audio container that helps preserve original audio codecs.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.srt.descrip: A general-purpose text subtitle format with the broadest compatibility for common players and subtitle platforms.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.ass.descrip: Useful for preserving subtitle styles, positioning, and effects in advanced subtitle workflows.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.ssa.descrip: Useful for compatibility with some older subtitle editing or playback workflows.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.vtt.descrip: A WebVTT subtitle format that fits web and HTML5 players.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.ttml.descri: A standardized text subtitle format for some platform exchange and publishing workflows.
common.outputFormat.subtitle.mks.descrip: A Matroska subtitle container that helps preserve the original subtitle encoding, including image subtitles.
